shapes.title: Dockerfile declares an image whose effective final USER is root
shapes.description: A Dockerfile (matching name 'Dockerfile' or 'Dockerfile.<name>') in production-context path declares a runtime stage whose effective final USER is root, with no explicit USER directive transitioning to a non-root user before CMD/ENTRYPOINT execution. The shape catches the failure mode where a container is deployed running as root (UID 0) in production, bypassing the principle of least privilege. First calibrated shape in verify's infrastructure-as-code vertical and the first calibrated shape outside the code-failure domain — proves the methodology generalizes to declarative-IaC analysis. Multi-stage Dockerfiles are handled per pre-registration (only runtime stage's USER configuration is evaluated). Production-context filter suppresses files in test/, examples/, fixtures/, samples/, docs/ paths and Dockerfiles with.test/.example/.dev/.local/.debug suffixes. Base-image default-user lookup uses a frozen v1 allowlist of known-root-default images (alpine, ubuntu, debian, python, golang, etc.) and known-non-root-default images (node, jenkins). Private-registry / unknown base images route to an ambiguity bucket.

tier_history.reason: Pre-registration committed at 5514a59 binding the rubric: Dockerfile detection, trigger predicate including base-image-default lookup, multi-stage handling, production-context filter, six FP buckets, nine AMB classes, 5 binding fixtures + 7 spot tests, append-by-default. First methodology-extension cycle outside the code-failure domain.

container-root-01-v2-iac-grafana-v1-2026-04-28-ratified: v2 cycle ratification under the project calibration doctrine strong-single-corpus rule. Grafana cleared blocking-band precision (12 true positives, 2 false positives, 9 ambiguous on 23 findings = 85.71%) with 39.13% ambiguity (under 40% cap). Reproduced byte-identically across v1 and v2 cycles, proving reproducibility. Tier shipped→calibrated, severity warning→warning per project promotion policy. 13th calibrated shape overall, first calibrated shape in infrastructure-as-code vertical, first calibrated shape outside code-failure domain. Methodology applied without amendment — same 8-phase cycle, same pre-registration structure, same classifier-consistency check, same provenance-digest discipline as the 12 prior code-failure cycles.

corpus_suitability.reason: Cleared blocking-band precision twice (v1 cycle and v2 cycle re-measurement). Public-base-image distribution (alpine, ubuntu, debian, golang) maps to v1 detector's allowlist. 12 true positives, 2 false positives, 9 ambiguous on 23 findings, 85.71% precision.

Held v1 on private-registry an ambiguity bucket saturation. 8 of 12 findings used registry.k8s.io/* base images that v1 allowlist does not recognize. Real ambiguity, correctly routed by classifier. Future v2 detector with kubernetes-private-registry resolution would convert most an ambiguity bucket to TP/explicit-non-root suppression.

Held v2 with same private-registry pattern as kubernetes (apache/airflow internal base images dominate an ambiguity bucket). 9 of 12 findings an ambiguity bucket. Confirms the private-registry pattern is structural across orchestration / workflow infrastructure projects.

known_issues: v1 base-image allowlist is frozen at the project's known-root-default list. Private-registry images (registry.k8s.io/*, gcr.io/*, quay.io/<org>/*, GitHub-org-published images, etc.) route to an ambiguity bucket when the runtime stage has no USER directive. This is a real ambiguity, not a defect — the detector correctly does not assume privacy-registry images run as root.
Multi-stage Dockerfiles where the runtime stage USES a stage-alias FROM (e.g., FROM build AS final) are handled by walking the alias chain; circular FROM AS chains are disallowed by Docker.
FROM scratch with runtime instructions but no USER routes to an ambiguity bucket. The scratch image has no documented default user; ambiguous.
ONBUILD USER directives route to an ambiguity bucket (rare; v1 doesn't model ONBUILD).
Build-arg-driven USER (USER ${RUNUSER}) routes to an ambiguity bucket.

Path-based FP heuristics (a false-positive bucket build-tooling, a false-positive bucket privileged-by-design, a false-positive bucket vendor) are heuristic and may misclassify edge cases. v2 may add Dockerfile-comment-based annotation parsing.
Containerfile (Podman alternative) is deferred to v2.

v1 corpus-pair attempts surfaced a pattern: orchestration/observability projects publish their own base images, which dominates an ambiguity bucket. End-user OSS apps that use public base images (grafana, alpine-based services) are the structurally-cleanest corpora for this shape under v1 doctrine.

shapes.title: Kubernetes manifest container missing resources.limits
shapes.description: A Kubernetes manifest YAML in production-context path declares a workload (Pod, Deployment, DaemonSet, StatefulSet, Job, CronJob, ReplicaSet, or ReplicationController) where a container in the relevant container path (containers, initContainers, ephemeralContainers, including under template.spec and jobTemplate.spec.template.spec) does not declare resources.limits with at least one of cpu or memory. The shape catches the failure mode where a container deployed without resource limits causes cluster-stability failures: noisy-neighbor resource starvation, OOM-kill cascades, and scheduler inability to apply QoS guarantees. Production-context filter (, inherited from K8S-PRIVILEGED-01) suppresses files in test/, examples/, fixtures/, samples/, docs/ paths and YAML files with.test/.example/.dev/.local/.debug suffixes. Helm Go-template files are skipped at detector level (would route an ambiguity bucket if classified). v1 is permissive on incomplete limits — cpu OR memory present is sufficient, since the failure mode is 'no limits at all' not 'incomplete limits'. v1 detector emits per-container findings: a manifest with N containers where M lack limits produces M findings.

tier_history.reason: Pre-registration committed at fa340d4 binding the rubric: YAML manifest detection, trigger predicate (— no resources / no limits / empty limits / cpu+memory both absent), multi-document handling, production-context filter, six FP buckets, nine AMB classes, 5 binding fixtures + 7 spot tests, deterministic-remediation declared true with canonical fix specified, append-by-default. Third k8s shape after K8S-PRIVILEGED-01 and CONTAINER-ROOT-01 v3; reuses YAML parser + container-traversal pattern unchanged. Sprint 2 of the IaC depth bet, pivoted from Terraform shapes after corpus-density finding.

k8s-missing-limits-01-v1-iac-argo-cd-v1-2026-04-29: ratification under the project calibration doctrine strong-single-corpus rule. argo-cd cleared the strong-single-corpus bar overwhelmingly: 149 true positives, 0 false positives, 0 ambiguous on 149-finding clean denominator = 100.00% precision, 0.00% ambiguity. argo-cd's manifests/ tree is the project's canonical install-manifest set (install.yaml, core-install.yaml, HA variants, ApplicationSet, redis HA, namespace-install). The project ships unbounded by design — operators are expected to add limits per cluster sizing — making 100% of production manifests genuine TPs under the predicate. Tier shipped→calibrated, severity uncalibrated→warning per project promotion policy. Fourth IaC calibration cycle, third k8s shape, validates that methodology generalizes through three k8s shapes with no rubric pollution. Companion corpus iac-k8s-manifests-v1 (kubernetes/kubernetes) did not promote with a non-promotion finding documentation: 53.57% precision is corpus-modeling failure (production-context filter did not enumerate kubernetes monorepo test-artifact paths under staging/src/k8s.io/<component>/artifacts/), not shape precision failure.

corpus_suitability.reason: Cleared strong-single-corpus bar at maximum precision: 149 true positives, 0 false positives, 0 ambiguous on 149-finding clean denominator = 100.00%. argo-cd manifests/ is the canonical OSS-published install tree shipping unbounded by design; every fire is a genuine TP under the predicate.

Held v1 on corpus-modeling gap, not shape precision failure. 53.57% precision driven by 15 true positives that are kubernetes monorepo test artifacts under staging/src/k8s.io/<component>/artifacts/ paths the pre-registration filter did not enumerate. A non-promotion finding for v2 detector evolution (the v2 methodology ): expand production-context filter to recognize monorepo test-artifact path conventions.

known_issues: v1 production-context filter does not enumerate kubernetes monorepo test-artifact path conventions (staging/src/<repo>/<component>/artifacts/, staging/src/<repo>/<component>/<package>/<file>.yaml). Kubernetes/kubernetes corpus did not promote with this a non-promotion finding; v2 candidate is to expand the filter or adopt a stricter 'production manifest' predicate requiring deployment markers (replica counts, namespace bindings, label selectors).
v1 is permissive on incomplete limits: cpu-only or memory-only counts as 'has limits'. A v2 may tighten this if the ambiguity benefit outweighs the precision-loss risk.
Helm Go-template files skipped at detector level. Templated YAMLs that don't expand to literal limits are out-of-scope for v1.
Kustomize strategic-merge patches route to an ambiguity bucket (v1 does not resolve overlays).
YAML anchor / merge-key references on resources route to an ambiguity bucket (v1 does not resolve aliases).
Custom Resource Definitions where 'resources' is a string identifier (e.g., resources: medium) route to an ambiguity bucket.
Determinism check passed in both corpora (mismatch_count=0 on 28 + 149 classifications).

shapes.title: Kubernetes manifest container missing both livenessProbe and readinessProbe
shapes.description: A Kubernetes manifest YAML in production-context path declares a workload (Pod, Deployment, DaemonSet, StatefulSet, ReplicaSet, ReplicationController) where a container in spec.containers declares NEITHER livenessProbe NOR readinessProbe with at least one probe-action (httpGet, tcpSocket, exec, grpc) populated. The shape catches the failure mode where a container deployed without health probes blackholes traffic to dead replicas — kubelet cannot determine container liveness, kube-proxy cannot exclude unready containers from Service endpoints, and silent outages result. Production-context filter (, inherited from K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01) suppresses files in test/, examples/, fixtures/, samples/, docs/ paths and YAML files with.test/.example/.dev/.local/.debug suffixes. Helm Go-template files are skipped at detector level. spec.initContainers and spec.ephemeralContainers are NOT walked (one-shot bootstrap/debug containers; probes don't apply by design per ). Job and CronJob workload kinds are excluded at detector level — one-shot containers exit on completion; probes are operationally meaningless. v1 detector emits per-container findings.

tier_history.reason: Pre-registration committed at b3feb96 binding the rubric: YAML manifest detection, trigger predicate (— no_probes if neither field declared, empty_probes if one or both declared but no probe-action populated), Job/CronJob exclusion at detector level, initContainers/ephemeralContainers exclusion, production-context filter, six FP buckets including a false-positive bucket expanded for probe-by-design exclusions (metric exporters, log shippers), nine AMB classes with an ambiguity bucket routing for empty/no-action probes, 5 binding fixtures + 9 spot tests, deterministic-remediation declared FALSE (probe action varies fundamentally by workload), append-by-default. Fifth IaC calibration cycle, fourth k8s shape, third under YAML parser primitive.

First two-corpus standard-path ratification of the IaC depth bet. the project calibration doctrine two-corpus warning band: argo-cd 41 true positives, 0 false positives, 0 ambiguous on 41-finding clean denominator = 100.00% precision (blocking band) and kubernetes/kubernetes 13 true positives, 12 false positives, 0 ambiguous on 25-finding clean denominator = 52.00% precision (warning band, +2 percentage points above the 50% threshold). Both corpora cleared floor + warning + ambiguity cap independently. Two-corpus blocking unavailable because companion kubernetes precision (52%) below the ≥80% blocking threshold required of both. project promotion policy to warning. Tier shipped→calibrated, severity uncalibrated→warning. Honest disclosure: kubernetes precision benefits from the corpus-modeling gap (12 of 13 kubernetes TPs are monorepo test-artifact paths under staging/src/k8s.io/<component>/artifacts/ that the filter does not enumerate); v2 corpus-modeling fix would re-measure but the v1 result stands honestly as recorded under the v1 rubric. argo-cd 100% on 41 findings is the uncontested canonical signal of the shape's actual precision when the corpus has clean production-manifest separation. 15th calibrated shape overall, fourth k8s shape, fifth IaC calibration cycle.

corpus_suitability.reason: Cleared blocking band at 100% precision on 41-finding clean denominator. argo-cd manifests/ tree and gitops-engine agent ship without probes by design — operators expected to add probes per cluster sizing and observability stack. Every fire is a genuine TP under the predicate. Zero an ambiguity bucket empty_probes findings — every probe declaration has at least one probe-action populated.

Cleared warning band at 52% precision on 25-finding clean denominator. Same a non-promotion finding as K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01: 12 of 13 true positives are kubernetes monorepo test artifacts under staging/src/k8s.io/<component>/artifacts/ paths the filter does not enumerate. 12 false positives cleanly classified by inherited rubric (7 a false-positive bucket cluster/addons/ build-tooling, 5 a false-positive bucket system-component workloads). v2 corpus-modeling fix would re-measure.

known_issues: Same corpus-modeling gap as K8S-MISSING-LIMITS-01: kubernetes monorepo test-artifact path convention (staging/src/<repo>/<component>/artifacts/) not enumerated. Inflates kubernetes corpus precision in v1; honest disclosure logged in v1 closeout.
v1 only walks spec.containers — initContainers and ephemeralContainers excluded by design per. v2 may add ephemeral-debug shape if substrate justifies.
Job and CronJob excluded at detector level. v2 may add a separate Job-failure shape (no restartPolicy, no activeDeadlineSeconds).

Probe action requirement (httpGet/tcpSocket/exec/grpc populated) is strict. Probes declared with only timing parameters route empty_probes → an ambiguity bucket. v2 may tighten to require initialDelaySeconds for slow-start workloads.
Helm Go-template files skipped at detector level. Templated YAMLs out-of-scope for v1.
Kustomize strategic-merge patches route to an ambiguity bucket.
YAML anchor / merge-key references on probe fields route to an ambiguity bucket.
Determinism check passed in both corpora (mismatch_count=0 on 41 + 25 classifications).
Cross-corpus aggregate precision (clean denom): 54 / 66 = 81.82%. Above blocking threshold in aggregate but two-corpus rule requires per-corpus ≥80%; not used for tier escalation.

shapes.title: GitHub Actions workflow references third-party action by tag rather than commit SHA
shapes.description: A GitHub Actions workflow file under.github/workflows/ references a third-party action (per first-party allowlist excluding actions/* and github/*) by tag (e.g., @v3, @v4.5.0) rather than by full 40-character commit SHA. The shape catches the supply-chain failure mode where a third-party action's tag is mutable; the action's owner can re-publish a tag pointing to compromised code, and downstream consumers running @v3 automatically pull and execute the compromised code with their secrets in scope (the 2025 tj-actions/changed-files compromise demonstrated this at scale). Production-context filter suppresses workflows with name field containing test/experimental/draft/playground tokens. Local references (./...), Docker image references (docker://...), first-party actions (actions/*, github/*), and SHA-pinned references are all excluded at detector level. v1 detector emits per-step findings with a ref_target_shape hint (tag/branch/short_sha/expression/matrix_ref) used by classifier for AMB routing. v2 classifier narrows the v1 an ambiguity bucket clause: major-version tags route TP rather than an ambiguity bucket, per industry consensus that tag-pinning is a real failure mode regardless of project release-policy claims.

tier_history.reason: V1 pre-registration committed at f6cce9d. New parser primitive (GitHub Actions workflow YAML walker, distinct from k8s YAML and Dockerfile parsers). New role-tagged corpora gha-calcom-v1 + gha-triggerdev-v1. First cross-parser-family substrate test of the IaC vertical.

V1 measurement did not promote with substrate finding: rubric an ambiguity bucket clause swallowed the entire TP signal because the empirical OSS GHA tag-pinned population is uniformly @vN-shaped (Marketplace convention). 42 of 57 v1 findings routed an ambiguity bucket. Cal.com 0/4/16 (precision 0%, ambiguity 80%, denom 4 below floor); trigger.dev 0/11/26 (precision 0%, ambiguity 70.27%, denom 11 above floor). Held under all three thresholds. Honest-discipline preserved per the v2 methodology frozen-rubric / invalidation: no mid-run rubric expansion. V2 dispatched immediately.

V2 ratification under the project calibration doctrine two-corpus standard-path warning band. Single-clause rubric narrowing per the v2 methodology detector evolution: v1 an ambiguity bucket major-version-tag clause removed; major-version tags route TP unless they hit a false-positive bucket (extended-trust publisher: docker/, aws-actions/, azure/, google-github-actions/, hashicorp/, pypa/) or an ambiguity bucket (Marketplace-verified-creator: codecov, snyk, sonarsource, slackapi, etc.). Cal.com 12/4/4 = 75.00% precision, 20.00% ambiguity. Trigger.dev 25/11/1 = 69.44% precision, 2.70% ambiguity. Both corpora cleared floor + warning + ambiguity cap independently. Two-corpus blocking unavailable (neither at 80%). project promotion policy to warning. Tier shipped→calibrated, severity uncalibrated→warning. V1→V2 reproducibility check PASSED on both corpora: v2 findings.jsonl byte-identical to v1 findings.jsonl (positive determinism across rubric-narrowing boundary; detector code reused byte-identically; only classifier branched). 16th calibrated shape overall, sixth IaC calibration cycle, first cross-parser-family ratification.

corpus_suitability.reason: Cleared warning band at 75% precision on 16-finding clean denominator. cal.com workflows (50 files, 194 uses, 7 SHA-pinned) provide high-density operator-configured signal. 12 true positives are major-version-tag references on non-allowlisted third-party publishers; 4 a false-positive bucket are extended-trust publishers (docker, hashicorp); 4 an ambiguity bucket are Marketplace-verified-creator publishers.

Cleared warning band at 69.44% precision on 36-finding clean denominator. trigger.dev workflows (21 files, 115 uses, 2 SHA-pinned) provide companion-corpus signal. 25 true positives major-version-tag references; 11 a false-positive bucket extended-trust publishers; 1 an ambiguity bucket Marketplace-verified-creator.

known_issues: v1 → v2 single-clause rubric evolution (within same session) demonstrates the v2 methodology detector evolution at its strongest. v1 honestly held; v2 ratified on a one-line classifier change with byte-identical detector reproducibility on findings.

Two-corpus blocking unavailable because neither corpus reached 80% precision. Cal.com 75% (4 a false-positive bucket extended-trust publishers limit precision); trigger.dev 69.44% (11 a false-positive bucket). A v3 cycle could narrow the extended-trust list (e.g., remove docker/* on the rationale that even Docker official actions have been compromised) but that's a separate substrate question.

First-party allowlist is conservative — actions/* and github/* only. Extended-trust list at a false-positive bucket (docker/, aws-actions/, azure/, google-github-actions/, hashicorp/, pypa/) is the v1 frozen scope; v2 may expand or contract based on threat-model evidence.

Marketplace-verified-creator allowlist at an ambiguity bucket is heuristic — v1 hard-codes a sample of widely-used verified creators (codecov, snyk, sonarsource, slackapi, gradle, jetbrains, cloudflare, goreleaser, softprops). v2 should query GitHub Marketplace API for canonical list (network call; would break determinism unless cached as a pinned snapshot dataset, similar to DEP-VULN-01's GHSA snapshot).

Branch references (@main, @master, @develop) route an ambiguity bucket — distinct from major-version-tag because branch is by-design mutable. v2 may route an ambiguity bucket as TP under a separate v3 cycle if substrate justifies.
Workflow-expression substitutions (${{ env.X }}) and matrix-strategy variables route an ambiguity bucket / an ambiguity bucket. v1 does not evaluate workflow expressions.
Composite action definitions (.github/actions/<name>/action.yml) and reusable workflow definitions are out of scope for v1.

Negative-control corpora documented for future v2 use: argo-cd 93% SHA-pinned (108/116), supabase 99% (163/164), sentry 80% (141/176), formbricks 81% (68/84). These corpora demonstrate the engine correctly stays quiet on disciplined supply chains.

Determinism: mismatch_count=0 on both corpora, both v1 and v2. v1→v2 findings.jsonl byte-equality check passed on both corpora — positive proof that rubric narrowing affected only classification, not detection.

shapes.title: Kubernetes manifest container has no effective non-root enforcement
shapes.description: A Kubernetes manifest YAML in production-context path declares a workload (Pod, Deployment, DaemonSet, StatefulSet, ReplicaSet, ReplicationController) where a container in spec.containers has no effective non-root enforcement — meaning neither container-level nor inherited Pod-level securityContext provides runAsNonRoot:true OR runAsUser:<non-zero>. Detector walks Pod-level and container-level securityContext, resolves inheritance per Kubernetes semantics (container-level overrides Pod-level), and handles container-level explicit-opt-out cases (runAsNonRoot:false or runAsUser:0 blocks Pod-level inheritance). Three missing_shape categories emitted: no_security_context, no_non_root_fields, explicit_root. Production-context filter (, inherited) suppresses test/examples/fixtures paths. Helm Go-template files skipped at detector level. Job and CronJob excluded at detector level (out of v1 scope). a false-positive bucket clause is BROADER than prior k8s shapes: includes monitoring agents (datadog-agent, prometheus-node-exporter, fluentd, fluent-bit, logstash, filebeat, metricbeat, newrelic), security tooling (falco, tetragon, security-agent), CNI/CSI drivers (calico-node, cilium, flannel, weave, csi-*), system components (kube-proxy, kubelet, etcd, coredns, etc.), AND any container under Pod-level hostNetwork/hostPID/hostIPC/hostPath (by-design privileged context). Most structurally complex k8s detector to date.

tier_history.reason: Pre-registration committed at d480f34. Multi-level securityContext walk + Pod-to-container inheritance + container-level override semantics + three missing_shape categories. Broader a false-positive bucket clause enumerated for monitoring-agent/security-tool/CNI populations. 5 binding fixtures + 15 spot tests; deterministic-remediation declared TRUE. Seventh IaC calibration cycle, fifth k8s shape, first dispatch from corpus survey substrate pool (commit 75de434).

Strong-single-corpus ratification per the project calibration doctrine. argo-cd 14 true positives, 0 false positives, 0 ambiguous on 14-finding clean denominator = 100.00% precision, 0.00% ambiguity. Companion corpus iac-datadog-agent-v1 did not promote with a non-promotion finding documentation EXACTLY AS PREDICTED by pre-registration (corpus selected because broader a false-positive bucket clause was load-bearing on monitoring-agent corpora; empirical 16 a false-positive bucket / 17 clean-denom matched prediction). project promotion policy to warning. Tier shipped→calibrated, severity uncalibrated→warning. 17th calibrated shape overall, fifth IaC k8s shape. Third consecutive operator-configured shape to ratify at 100% on argo-cd (after MISSING-LIMITS 149/0/0 and MISSING-PROBES 41/0/0).

corpus_suitability.reason: Cleared blocking band at 100% precision on 14-finding clean denominator. argo-cd manifests/ tree continues operator-configured pattern: containers ship without securityContext by design; operator is expected to apply Pod Security Standards or admission policies. Every fire is a genuine TP.

did not promote at 5.88% precision with a non-promotion finding documentation. Corpus selected specifically because broader a false-positive bucket clause was load-bearing here; empirical 16 a false-positive bucket / 17 clean-denom confirms classifier correctly routes monitoring-agent containers (datadog-agent uses runAsUser:0 + hostPID:true legitimately). The 1 TP is a generic nginx HPA example without securityContext. This is the a false-positive bucket clause working as designed; held-row is honest substrate evidence, not failure.

known_issues: Same corpus-modeling gap as MISSING-LIMITS / MISSING-PROBES (kubernetes monorepo staging/src/.../artifacts/ paths). Documented; v2 candidate.
v1 only walks spec.containers per; initContainers/ephemeralContainers excluded.
Job and CronJob excluded at detector level.

Multi-field, multi-level predicate is the most structurally complex k8s detector to date. Container-level override semantics (runAsNonRoot:false OR runAsUser:0 blocks Pod-level inheritance) caught and fixed at fixture-time before measurement (real predicate-design bug surfaced through binding-fixture discipline).

a false-positive bucket clause is broader than MISSING-LIMITS/MISSING-PROBES because security-context predicate has wider by-design FP cluster. Enumerated explicitly in pre-registration. Heuristic; v2 may refine based on additional substrate.

an ambiguity bucket routes no_non_root_fields cases (operator declared securityContext but only set capabilities/allowPrivilegeEscalation/readOnlyRootFilesystem fields). 9 such findings on datadog-agent corpus.
Helm template skip; Kustomize → an ambiguity bucket; YAML anchor → an ambiguity bucket.
Determinism: mismatch_count=0 on both corpora.
explicit_root finding shape (runAsUser:0 or runAsNonRoot:false) treated equally with no_security_context for promotion (both represent same structural failure).

shapes.title: Kubernetes Pod-template container uses:latest tag or no tag
shapes.description: A Kubernetes manifest YAML in production-context path declares a workload (Pod, Deployment, DaemonSet, StatefulSet, ReplicaSet, ReplicationController) where a container in spec.containers references an image with `image: <repo>:latest` or `image: <repo>` (no tag, which Kubernetes implicit-defaults to:latest). The deployed container is whatever the registry's:latest tag points to at pull time, which can change between identical re-deploys. First k8s shape in verify substrate with string-pattern predicate (image tag check). Detector handles digest pinning (@sha256:...) as suppression, registry-with-port edge cases (registry.io:5000/org/repo:tag), and semantic version tags. Production-context filter suppresses test/examples/fixtures paths. Pre-reg explicitly enumerated a false-positive bucket cluster for argo-cd resource_customizations/*/health_test.yaml pattern (49 raw matches anticipated and handled). Job and CronJob excluded at detector level. a false-positive bucket narrowed to documentation/example workloads only (no by-design legitimate:latest production use case).

tier_history.reason: Pre-registration committed at 2a670a4. First k8s shape with string-pattern predicate (image tag check). FP buckets DELIBERATELY NARROW per pre-reg note (lesson from K8S-a prior cycle-SA-TOKEN-01 v1): a false-positive bucket explicitly enumerates argo-cd resource_customizations test-data pattern; a false-positive bucket narrowed to documentation/example workloads only; AMB clauses limited to template/expression/anchor cases. 5 binding fixtures + 15 spot tests; deterministic-remediation declared TRUE. Ninth IaC calibration cycle, sixth k8s shape, second dispatch from corpus survey substrate pool.

Strong-single-corpus ratification per the project calibration doctrine. argo-cd 62 true positives, 0 false positives, 0 ambiguous on 62-finding clean denominator = 100.00% precision, 0.00% ambiguity. CLEAREST IaC ratification of session in TP/false-positive and ambiguity distribution. Pre-reg a false-positive bucket enumeration empirically validated: anticipated 49 raw matches in resource_customizations/*/health_test.yaml were caught via testdata path + a false-positive bucket regex routing. lessons from a prior cycle applied successfully — zero clause-swallow problem. Companion datadog-agent below floor (clean denom 1) but single finding correctly routed a false-positive bucket. Tier shipped→calibrated, severity uncalibrated→warning. 18th calibrated shape overall. FOURTH consecutive operator-configured shape to ratify at 100% on argo-cd (after MISSING-LIMITS 149/0/0, MISSING-PROBES 41/0/0, MISSING-SECURITY-CONTEXT 14/0/0).

corpus_suitability.reason: Cleared blocking band at 100% precision on 62-finding clean denominator. argo-cd manifests/ and gitops-engine/agent/manifests/ trees ship with image: <repo>:latest because release process substitutes:vX.Y.Z at publish time. On the development SHA measured, every fire is a genuine TP under the predicate.

Held below floor (clean denom 1). The single finding (Dockerfiles/manifests/hpa-example/nginx.yaml) correctly routed a false-positive bucket. Pre-reg predicted low-density companion (1-2 candidates); empirical 1 matched prediction exactly. datadog-agent uses pinned image tags throughout production manifests.

known_issues: Same corpus-modeling gap as MISSING-LIMITS / MISSING-PROBES / MISSING-SECURITY-CONTEXT (kubernetes monorepo staging/src/.../artifacts/ paths). Documented; v2 candidate.
v1 only walks spec.containers; initContainers/ephemeralContainers excluded.
Job and CronJob excluded at detector level.
String-pattern predicate handles standard image format [registry[:port]/]repo[:tag][@digest]. Unusual structures (multiple @ signs, multiple: after last /) route an ambiguity bucket defensively.
a false-positive bucket narrowed to documentation/example workloads only — no by-design legitimate:latest production use case.

a false-positive bucket explicitly enumerates argo-cd resource_customizations/*/(health_test|health_status|actions|wait_for|app_action).yaml pattern via regex (these files look like k8s manifests but are health-detection engine test data).
Helm template skip; Kustomize → an ambiguity bucket; YAML anchor → an ambiguity bucket.
Determinism: mismatch_count=0 on both corpora.
Pre-reg note explicitly references a prior cycle lesson and keeps false-positive and ambiguity clauses narrow. Empirical: zero clause-swallow.

shapes.title: Dockerfile FROM directive references an external image without a content-addressed digest pin
shapes.description: A Dockerfile FROM directive references an external literal base image (not scratch, not a stage-alias, not ARG-driven) without a @sha256:<digest> content-addressed pin. The image can move behind the same Dockerfile, so the build is not byte-reproducible across re-pulls. v1 detector excludes ARG-driven FROMs at detector level (suppressed_arg_base counter) on substrate evidence from moby corpus where build-args dominate; classifier handles FP buckets including operator override comments, generated Dockerfiles, builder-only stages with digest-pinned runtime, and gcr.io/distroless nonroot tags. Risk family: supply-chain drift; pairs with GHA-SHA-PIN-01 which catches the same class in CI workflow YAML.

tier_history.reason: Pre-registration committed binding the rubric: trigger predicate (S2.2 -- external + literal + missing @sha256 + production-context), detector-level scope for ARG / scratch / stage-alias / templated files (S2.6, NOT classifier ambiguity), six FP buckets (S4) including builder-only with digest-pinned runtime + distroless nonroot tags + operator overrides + generated Dockerfiles, six AMB classes (S5) with private-registry routing to an ambiguity bucket, 12 binding fixtures + 7 mechanical fixtures (S10), deterministic-remediation declared true (S13) with replace_literal + computed-digest template, append-by-default (S11). Eighth IaC calibration cycle, second Dockerfile-surface shape after CONTAINER-ROOT-01, selected by survey-iac-tier2-density.ts pivot from a prior survey Terraform after Terraform corpus availability blocked a future release.

Two-corpus standard-path ratification. iac-grafana-v1 (primary): 30 true positives, 0 false positives, 0 ambiguous on 30-finding clean denominator = 100.00% precision, 0.00% ambiguity. iac-airflow-v1 (companion): 14 true positives, 0 false positives, 0 ambiguous on 14-finding clean denominator = 100.00% precision, 0.00% ambiguity. Both corpora cleared blocking-band precision (>=80%) and ambiguity cap (<=40%) independently. project promotion policy applies: severity stays warning. Tier shipped -> calibrated, CI behavior not-yet-deployed -> warning-only. 12th IaC vertical promotion, second Dockerfile-surface promotion after CONTAINER-ROOT-01, first calibrated shape in the supply-chain drift risk family on the Dockerfile surface (pairs with GHA-SHA-PIN-01 which covers CI workflow YAML).

corpus_suitability.reason: Survey 2026-05-02 returned lit_fire=30 / amb=7 (19% AMB), well under the 40% cap. Strong-single-corpus path is plausible if companion clears.
Survey 2026-05-02 returned lit_fire=15 / amb=10 (40% AMB exactly at the cap). Threshold companion.

Held out of v1. Survey returned 80% AMB (40 of 50) driven by build-arg FROMs. v1 detector excludes ARG-driven FROMs at detector level (S2.6); a v2 cycle should re-measure moby once that suppression is confirmed against this corpus.

known_issues: v1 a false-positive bucket internal-controlled-registry allowlist is empty by design; every private-registry FROM routes an ambiguity bucket unless an operator override comment is present. v2 may add audited registry+org combinations.

a false-positive bucket distroless nonroot/debug tags are classified as FP at v1 rather than detector-suppressed. Google documents these tag suffixes as effectively immutable; v2 audit may convert to detector-level suppression.
Multi-arch manifest list semantics are not modeled. A FROM with --platform= is parsed as a single image; cross-arch digest drift is out of v1 scope (an ambiguity bucket).
ONBUILD chain references in parent images are not resolved (an ambiguity bucket); v1 honors only the source text.

Project-documentation-declared immutability (e.g., README states tag stability) is not detected (an ambiguity bucket); only the per-FROM and file-level # verify:exception:digest-pin comment forms are honored.
